info.title: smirk-backend-core
info.description: Open, self-hostable backend for the Smirk non-custodial multi-chain wallet.

get.description:
Looks up the user by lowercased `?name=` and, if they have a linked
`nostr_pubkey`, returns it in `names` (with the default relay hints under
`relays`). An unknown name, an unlinked user, or an absent `name` parameter
all return an empty document — this endpoint never errors on the lookup path
and never discloses which usernames exist.

post.summary: Check whether a wallet restore is valid (created here + keys match).
post.description:
Two governors run BEFORE any user lookup: a per-IP limit
([`RESTORE_IP_THRESHOLD`]) that bounds distinct-fingerprint scanning, and a
per-fingerprint failure limit ([`RESTORE_FAIL_THRESHOLD`]); either trips a
429. The per-IP limit uses [`client_ip`] so an untrusted `X-Forwarded-For`
cannot evade it. Every attempt is recorded (peppered fingerprint, salted IP).

The known/unknown branches are equalized: the unknown branch runs the same
key-comparison loop against an empty stored set, so the two paths do the same
work and the response does not become a timing oracle for existence beyond the
already-intentional `exists` field.

post.summary: Register a new extension wallet or authenticate an existing one.
post.description:
The BTC pubkey hash is the unique identity. A signed timestamp proves control
of the BTC private key (defeats registration with a stolen public key). When
the PoW gate applies and the wallet is new, a valid `altcha_solution` is
required; a returning user (a known `pubkey_hash`) bypasses PoW.

## Derivation rotation is authenticated (account-takeover defense)

A known `seed_fingerprint` at a NEW `pubkey_hash` (the wallet changed its
derivation scheme) may re-point the existing user row — but ONLY when the
request also carries a `rotation_signature` that verifies against the BTC key
already on file for that user (proving control of the seed-derived key, not
merely knowledge of the fingerprint, which `check_restore` discloses and is
not secret). A bare fingerprint match WITHOUT a valid rotation proof is
treated as a brand-new identity: a fresh user row is created on the new
`pubkey_hash` and the matched victim row is never modified. The rotation path
is gated by PoW exactly like any other new-pubkey registration.

post.summary:
Revoke the session backing a refresh token. Idempotent: an unknown or
already-revoked token still returns success (logout should never error).
Sign in with a Nostr identity (NIP-98 HTTP Auth, login grade).
post.description:
The `Nostr <base64(event)>` token is read from the `Authorization` header and
verified against the canonical `config.identity.public_api_url` + `/auth/nostr`
(never the request Host). The npub must ALREADY be linked to a user (see
[`nostr_link`]); this endpoint NEVER creates a user — an unlinked npub is 401.

200.description: Session for the linked Nostr identity

401.description: Invalid NIP-98 token or no linked account

post.summary: Link a Nostr identity (npub) to the authenticated user.
post.description:
Dual auth: the Bearer JWT identifies the user; a NIP-98 *signed-action* proof
(not a login token) proves control of the npub AND binds a server-issued
single-use `nonce`, the purpose `nostr_link`, and this exact request via the
request-descriptor hash. On success the npub is stored so a later
[`nostr_login`] resolves to the same wallet; a collision is 409.

## Descriptor binding is a CONTRACT (not an implementation detail)

The `payload` tag binds `descriptor_sha256(request_descriptor("POST",
"/api/v1/auth/nostr/link", "", b""))` — an EMPTY body hash, with no query.
The JSON `{nostr_token, nonce}` rides in the HTTP body but is deliberately NOT
part of the signed descriptor (it carries the proof itself, so it cannot also
be inside it). The wallet MUST build the identical descriptor. This exact
method/path/query/empty-body shape is a cross-impl contract and must be pinned
in a shared test vector (mirroring the nip98.rs interop test) before
`consume_link_nonce` is un-stubbed — otherwise the binding silently breaks or
weakens at integration time.

TODO(operator-surface): the server-nonce *issue* (`GET
/auth/nostr/link-challenge`, rand 32 bytes, stored) + the ATOMIC single-use
*consume* (a `DELETE ... WHERE nonce=$1 RETURNING` so a replay loses the race,
never check-then-delete), plus the wallet's signed-action token builder, are
wired in the operator-surface phase. Until then [`consume_link_nonce`] always
returns `false` and this handler FAILS CLOSED: it validates the full proof
shape but refuses every link, because it cannot yet prove the nonce was issued
by this server and is unused.

409.description: npub already linked to another account

post.summary:
Issue a fresh proof-of-work challenge for the wallet to solve before calling
`/auth/extension`. The solved payload is sent back as the request's
`altcha_solution`.
post.description:
Stateless: the challenge embeds an HMAC signature over its own fields plus an
expiry, so no issued-challenge store is needed. See [`crate::core::pow`].

post.summary: Rotate a refresh token.
post.description:
Verifies the JWT, then re-looks-up the ACTIVE session by peppered hash
(`get_session_by_token_hash` already filters revoked/expired) — a missing row
means the token was revoked, expired, or already rotated, and is rejected.
The session's `user_id` is asserted equal to the JWT `sub` (defense-in-depth
against any future token-confusion class). The old session is revoked and a
fresh pair issued (revoke-then-issue); the `revoke_session` race-loser also
rejects, so a stolen token cannot be reused.

post.summary: Issue a website-auth challenge bound to the requesting origin.
post.description:
The challenge is stored in `state.web_challenges` keyed by its own nonce and
expires shortly (see [`WebChallenge`]). The returned `challenge` message is
what the wallet signs; `challenge_id` is the nonce to present at verify.

post.summary: Verify a website-auth signature and mint a `Platform::Web` session.
post.description:
The challenge is CONSUMED (removed from the map) before any signature work,
so it is strictly single-use: a replayed `challenge_id` — or the loser of two
concurrent verifies — finds nothing and is rejected. After consume we check
expiry, verify the signature over the challenge message with the algorithm
for the submitted asset, resolve the ALREADY-registered user by the proven
key's `pubkey_hash` (never creating one), and issue a session. All failure
messages are literals (no oracle).

401.description: Invalid/expired challenge, bad signature, or unknown wallet

get.summary: Describe this instance's enabled chains and features.

get.description:
Returns `200` with `{ "status": "ok" }` whenever the server is up. Used by
load balancers and uptime checks; requires no authentication.

post.summary: Register or update one of the authenticated user's per-asset public keys.
post.description:
Idempotent: the DB upsert keys on `(user_id, asset, key_type)`, so re-posting
the same asset replaces the stored key material in place.

get.summary: Current fiat prices for the enabled feeds.

404.description: Price feed disabled on this server

get.summary: Resolve a username to its user and that user's receiving public keys.
get.description:
Public: this is how a sender discovers where to send. The lookup is
case-insensitive (the handle is canonicalized lowercase). An unknown handle
returns the same constant-shape response with `registered: false`.

post.summary: Claim or update the authenticated user's username.
post.description:
The submitted value is lowercased, validated, and checked against the
reserved list before the DB write. The UNIQUE constraint is the atomic claim:
a collision (taken or reserved) is a 409, never a 500.

get.summary: List a user's per-asset receiving public keys.
get.description: Public: a sender fetches these to construct a payment to `user_id`.

get.summary: Fetch a user's receiving public key for one asset.

200.description: The user's key for this asset

404.description: User has no key for this asset

post.summary: Broadcast a finalized, signed Grin transaction.

get.summary: Current Grin chain tip height (for confirmation counting).

post.summary: Cancel a relay (sender or recipient), unless already finalized.

404.description: Relay not found, not yours, or feature disabled

post.summary: Post a slatepack addressed to a registered recipient (sender = caller).

Record that the sender finalized + broadcast the transaction (sender only).
The backend does NOT finalize or broadcast — the wallet does that locally and
reports the resulting tx hash here.

404.description: Relay not found, not yours, or feature disabled

post.summary:
Poll a relay's current state (sender or recipient). The sender uses this to
fetch the recipient's response once status is `pending_sender`.

404.description: Relay not found, not yours, or feature disabled

get.summary: The caller's inbox: relays awaiting their response.

post.summary: Attach the recipient's response, advancing to `pending_sender`.

404.description: Relay not found, not yours, or feature disabled

409.description: Relay is not awaiting a recipient response

post.summary: View-only scan for the outputs a `rewind_hash` recognizes.
Balance + scan state for an account.

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset, address, or view key

post.summary: Confirmation count for a transaction (daemon).
Current chain height (daemon) for confirmation counting.
Transaction history (confirmed + mempool) for an account.

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset, address, or view key

post.summary: Random decoy outputs for ring construction.

Register an account (its view key) with the LWS so it begins scanning.
Idempotent at the LWS; pass `start_height` to scan from a wallet birthday.

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset, address, or view key

post.summary: Broadcast a finalized, signed Monero/Wownero transaction.

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset or malformed tx hex

post.summary: Unspent outputs for an account (for client-side spend construction).

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset, address, or view key

post.summary: Confirmed/unconfirmed balance for a BTC/LTC address.
Broadcast a finalized, signed BTC/LTC transaction.

400.description: Invalid/disabled asset or malformed tx hex

post.summary: Fee-rate estimate (sat/vB) for confirmation within `blocks` blocks.
Confirmed + mempool transaction history for a BTC/LTC address.
Best-chain tip height (for client-side confirmation counting).
Unspent outputs for a BTC/LTC address.

AddressRequest.description: An address query for a UTXO asset.

address.description: The address to query (validated by the Electrum client).

AssetPublicKey.description: A per-asset public key submitted by the wallet.

AssetSignature.description: One asset's signature over the challenge message.

asset.description: Asset whose key produced the signature (`btc`, `ltc`, `xmr`, `wow`, `grin`).

public_key.description: The public key that produced the signature (format depends on asset).

signature.description:
Signature over the challenge message. BIP-137 base64 for BTC/LTC; 64-byte
hex Ed25519 for XMR/WOW/Grin.

AuthResponse.description: Successful session response: a JWT pair plus minimal user info.

is_new.description: `true` when this request created the user (extension registration only).

BalanceResponse.description:
Confirmed/unconfirmed balance in satoshis. The client computes any total
(kept as separate integer fields — no lossy server-side sum).

BroadcastRequest.description: A broadcast request: a finalized, signed raw transaction.

contract_version.description: Capabilities contract version (additive changes do not bump it).

ChainCapability.description:
Per-chain availability. `network` is the configured network for UTXO chains
(so the wallet derives addresses for the right one); `null` for chains whose
network isn't a backend setting.

CheckRestoreRequest.description:
Ask whether a wallet (by seed fingerprint) was created on this backend, and
whether the submitted keys match.

keys.description: Submitted per-asset public keys, for verification.

CheckRestoreResponse.description:
Restore-check result. Constant-shape: the same fields are always present so
the response is not a structure oracle, and `user_id` is never returned (no
enumeration).

NOTE: `exists` is an INTENTIONAL disclosure (the wallet uses it to decide
whether to offer a restore). It is throttled per fingerprint AND per IP, and
nothing downstream treats fingerprint-existence as authority — the
derivation-rotation path in [`extension_register`] requires a key-control
proof, not a bare fingerprint.

exists.description: Whether the fingerprint exists on this backend.

keys_valid.description:
Whether every submitted key matches the stored key. `None` when the
fingerprint does not exist.

wow_start_height.description: WOW scan-start height, if the wallet was created here.

xmr_start_height.description: XMR scan-start height, if the wallet was created here.

confirmations.description: Confirmations, or `null` if the tx is unknown / not yet in a block.

CreateRelayRequest.description: Create a relay: post an (encrypted) slatepack addressed to a registered user.

amount_nanogrin.description: Amount in nanogrin (informational; the real amount is in the slate).

slate_id.description: The Grin slate id (the transaction's slate UUID).

slatepack.description: The armored slatepack (encrypted to the recipient).

ExtensionRegisterRequest.description: Register a new extension wallet or re-authenticate an existing one.

altcha_solution.description:
Optional proof-of-work solution. Required when the PoW gate applies to
this pubkey (see [`pow_applies`]); otherwise ignored.

keys.description:
Public keys for each supported asset. A `btc` key is required (it is the
identity).

rotation_signature.description:
Derivation-rotation proof: BIP-137 base64 signature of the SAME
`smirk-auth-{signed_timestamp}` message under the BTC key ALREADY ON FILE
for the user identified by `seed_fingerprint`. Required to re-point an
existing user row; without it (or if it does not verify against the stored
key) a fingerprint match is treated as a brand-new identity and the
existing row is never touched. See [`extension_register`].

seed_fingerprint.description:
Seed fingerprint `hex(SHA256(SHA256(seed))[..])`. Used for restore and to
LOCATE a candidate user row for the derivation-rotation path. By itself it
is NOT authority: a rotation also requires `rotation_signature` below.

signature.description:
BIP-137 base64 signature of `smirk-auth-{signed_timestamp}` under the
SUBMITTED (new) BTC key. Proves control of the key in `keys`.

signed_timestamp.description: Unix seconds that were signed to prove BTC key ownership.

wallet_birthday.description: Wallet creation time (unix seconds), to bound chain scans.

blocks.description: Target confirmation within this many blocks.

sat_per_vb.description: Estimated fee rate in sat/vB, or `null` if the server can't estimate.

FinalizeRelayRequest.description: Record that the sender finalized + broadcast the transaction.

tx_hash.description: The broadcast transaction hash/kernel (recorded for reference).

GrinBroadcastRequest.description: Broadcast a finalized, signed Grin transaction (built + signed by the wallet).

tx.description: The finalized transaction object (grin node `push_transaction` input).

GrinOutput.description: A single output recovered by a view-only scan. Amounts are nanogrin.

GrinScanRequest.description:
A view-only scan request. Carries the `rewind_hash` view credential, so it
deliberately omits `Debug` (never logged).

rewind_hash.description: The wallet's `rewind_hash` (64 hex). Forwarded to grin-wallet; not stored.

start_height.description: Scan from this block height (wallet birthday / last scanned). Omit for full.

GrinScanResponse.description: Result of a view-only scan: recognized outputs, total, and the resume index.

last_pmmr_index.description: Resume point (`last_pmmr_index`) for the next incremental scan.

status.description: Always `"ok"` when the service is reachable.

LookupUsernameResponse.description:
Result of resolving a username. Constant-shape: the same fields are always
present, so the response is not a structure oracle.

oneOf.description: The user's per-asset receiving public keys, if registered.

registered.description: Whether a user owns this username.

user_id.description: The resolved user id (UUID string), if registered.

LwsBalanceResponse.description:
Balance + scan state. `total_received` is the balance (an LWS cannot confirm
spends without the spend key; the wallet subtracts its own known spends).

username.description: The caller's username, or `null` if they have not claimed one.

NostrLinkRequest.description:
Link a Nostr identity to the authenticated user. This is a STATE CHANGE, so
it carries a signed-action proof, not a login-grade token.

nonce.description:
The server-issued single-use nonce the signed action must bind (the
event's `challenge` tag).

nostr_token.description:
The `Nostr <base64(event)>` signed-action token proving control of the
npub AND committing to the server nonce + this request.

currency.description: Fiat currency the quotes are in (e.g. `"usd"`).

prices.description: Asset symbol → price in `currency`. Only enabled feeds appear.

updated_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp of the last successful refresh; `null` until the first.

PublicKeysInfo.description: A user's per-asset receiving public keys, by asset.

RandomOutsRequest.description: Request decoy outputs for ring construction.

amounts.description: Amounts to request decoys for (`["0"]` for RingCT).

count.description: Decoys per real output (protocol ring size; clamped server-side).

RegisterKeyRequest.description: Register or update one of the caller's per-asset public keys.

public_key.description: The public key (format depends on the asset).

public_spend_key.description: XMR/WOW only: the public spend key.

RegisterRequest.description: Register (or import-with-height) an account for LWS scanning.

start_height.description: Scan from this block height (wallet birthday). Omit to scan from now.

view_key.description: Private view key (64 hex). Forwarded to the LWS; never stored or logged.

RelayEntry.description: A relay entry as returned to an authorized party.

response_slatepack.description: The recipient's response slatepack, once provided.

slatepack_content.description: The sender's armored slatepack (what the recipient responds to).

RespondRelayRequest.description: The recipient's response to a pending relay.

response_slatepack.description: The armored response slatepack (recipient's partial signature added).

username.description:
Desired username (3-32 chars, lowercase `[a-z0-9_]`, no leading/trailing
underscore). Submitted values are lowercased before validation.

SlateIdRequest.description: Identify a relay by its slate id (poll / cancel).

SpentOutputDto.description: A candidate spent output (verify with the spend key before trusting).

TxDto.description: A transaction in the account's history.

UnspentOutputDto.description: An unspent output for spend construction.

spend_key_images.description: Key images seen on-chain that may correspond to this output being spent.

UserInfo.description: Minimal, non-enumerable user info returned to a signed-in client.

nostr_pubkey.description: Linked Nostr pubkey (x-only hex), if any.

public_spend_key.description: XMR/WOW only: the public spend key.

UserKeysResponse.description: A user's full set of per-asset public keys.

ViewRequest.description: Address + private view key for a per-account query.

view_key.description: Private view key (64 hex). Forwarded to the LWS; never stored or logged.

WebsiteChallengeRequest.description: Request a website-auth challenge for a calling origin.

origin.description:
Origin of the requesting website (e.g. `https://smirk.cash`). Bound into
the challenge message so a signature for one origin is not reusable at
another.

WebsiteChallengeResponse.description: The challenge to sign, with the handle and expiry the wallet echoes back.

challenge.description: The exact message string to sign with the wallet's key.

challenge_id.description: Opaque handle to present at verify time (this is the challenge nonce).

WebsiteVerifyRequest.description: Verify a website-auth challenge with a single asset signature.

signature.description: The wallet's signature over the challenge message.

WellKnownResponse.description:
NIP-05 well-known document.

* `names` maps each resolved local part to its x-only Nostr pubkey (hex).
* `relays` maps each returned pubkey to a list of recommended relay URLs.

Both maps are empty when the name is unknown, unlinked, or omitted.

names.description: `{ local_part: nostr_pubkey_hex }`. Empty when nothing resolved.

bearer_auth.description: Session access token issued by the /auth/* endpoints.

tags.name: prices
tags.description: Fiat price feed (cached; per-feed configurable; feature-flagged).

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Wallet authentication: registration, restore, refresh, sessions, Nostr identity.

tags.name: btc_ltc
tags.description: Bitcoin/Litecoin chain access via Electrum (balance, UTXOs, history, tip, fee, broadcast).

tags.name: xmr_wow
tags.description: Monero/Wownero chain access via LWS (register, balance, history, unspent/random outs, submit, height, confirmations).

tags.name: grin
tags.description: Grin view-only chain access (rewind_hash scan, tip height, broadcast).

tags.name: grin_relay
tags.description: Grin slatepack relay: async non-custodial mailbox for interactive Grin transfers (feature-flagged).
